title: fix_pipeline_context_response
description: Response schema for the fix_pipeline_context agent that gathers pipeline failure context, classifies the failure, and decides whether a fix can be attempted.

failure_category.description: The category of the pipeline failure. Use the most specific category that fits.

suggested_fix_type.description: The suggested type of fix for the pipeline failure.

decision.description: Use 'create_plan' when confident a fix can be created and validation via local execution is warranted. Use 'direct_code_suggestions' ONLY when (a) a merge_request_url is provided AND (b) confidence is 'high' AND (c) the fix is narrow enough that the model can write the corrected lines straight from the summary without executing them; the flow then bypasses execution and posts inline suggestions directly. Use 'add_comment' when not confident or unable to create a fix. Use 'no_action' when the failure is a flaky test or infrastructure issue that requires no intervention.

confidence.description: Set 'high' for narrow, well-understood fixes (e.g. lint_failure or ci_config_error on a single file with the failing rule visible in the trace). Set 'low' for multi-file test failures, build failures, anything with insufficient_context, or whenever the agents_dot_md content directs you to always validate. Only 'high' permits 'direct_code_suggestions'.

summary.description: Self-contained description of the failing job(s), the observed error(s), the identified root cause, and any files or lines implicated. Downstream components rely on this summary instead of the gathered context, so it must include every detail needed to plan, explain, or execute the fix.
